articles.headline: Supreme Court Clarifies Scope of Legislative Immunity for MPs/MLAs in Corruption Cases
articles.explainer:
• A landmark Supreme Court ruling today delimited the scope of legislative immunity provided under Articles 105(2) and 194(2) of the Constitution.
• The Court held that immunity against prosecution for speeches and votes within the legislature does not extend to acts of bribery or corruption related to such actions.
• The judgment emphasizes the need for accountability and transparency among elected representatives.
• This decision is expected to have significant implications for anti-corruption efforts and the conduct of legislative business.
• It follows a series of petitions challenging the interpretation of parliamentary privileges in cases involving alleged 'cash-for-vote' or 'cash-for-query' scams.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Governance, Transparency and Accountability
prelimsPoints: Articles 105 and 194 of the Indian Constitution deal with powers, privileges, etc., of Members of Parliament and State Legislatures, respectively.
The current ruling distinguishes between legitimate legislative acts and acts of corruption.
The concept of 'privilege motion' and 'breach of privilege' are related to legislative immunity.
The judgement re-affirms the principle of accountability for public servants.
mainsPoints: Discuss the balance between ensuring legislative freedom and holding elected representatives accountable for corrupt practices.
Analyze the implications of this judgment on parliamentary conduct, electoral ethics, and the fight against corruption in India.
Evaluate how this ruling strengthens democratic principles by promoting transparency and integrity in legislative bodies.
Examine the historical context of parliamentary privileges and how judicial interpretations have shaped their application over time.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel striking a sound block in front of the Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: The first 'cash-for-vote' scandal in India dates back to 1993, involving the Narasimha Rao government.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Powers, Privileges and Immunities of MPs/MLAs

articles.headline: Economic Survey 2025-26 Projects Robust Growth Amidst Global Headwinds, Focus on Green Transition
articles.explainer:
• The annual Economic Survey, presented a day before the Union Budget, projected India's GDP growth at 7.2-7.8% for the upcoming fiscal year.
• It highlighted resilient domestic demand, public capital expenditure, and strong services sector performance as key growth drivers.
• The survey called for increased investment in green technologies and sustainable infrastructure to achieve net-zero targets.
• It also emphasized the need for continued structural reforms in agriculture, labour, and logistics to enhance productivity and competitiveness.
• Inflation management, fiscal consolidation, and job creation were identified as critical policy priorities.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Investment models
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Economic Survey is prepared by the Chief Economic Adviser (CEA) and presented by the Finance Minister.
It provides a detailed report on the state of the Indian economy in the financial year gone by.
The survey often outlines policy recommendations and future outlook.
Green transition involves shifting to sustainable and low-carbon economic activities.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key recommendations of the Economic Survey 2025-26 regarding India's path to a sustainable and high-growth economy.
Discuss the challenges posed by global headwinds and domestic structural issues in achieving the projected GDP growth targets.
Evaluate the role of public capital expenditure and green investments in driving India's economic recovery and long-term development.
Examine the interlinkages between economic growth, environmental sustainability, and social equity as highlighted in the survey.
articles.imageDescription: A graph showing India's projected GDP growth curve with green energy infrastructure in the background.
articles.trivia: The first Economic Survey was presented in 1950-51 and was part of the Union Budget documents until 1964.

articles.headline: Tragic Fire at Kolkata Restaurant Claims 25 Lives, Raises Concerns Over Urban Safety Norms
articles.explainer:
• A devastating fire erupted at a popular Wow! Momo restaurant in Kolkata's bustling Park Street area, resulting in the death of at least 25 people.
• The incident, which occurred during peak evening hours, trapped many customers and staff members inside the multi-storey building.
• Initial investigations point towards a short circuit as the probable cause, exacerbated by inadequate fire safety measures and emergency exits.
• The tragedy has sparked widespread public outcry and renewed calls for stricter enforcement of fire safety regulations and urban planning codes in commercial establishments.
• State authorities have ordered a high-level inquiry and announced compensation for the victims' families.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Disaster and disaster management
Role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Safety and security issues
prelimsPoints: National Building Code of India (NBC) provides guidelines for building construction and fire safety.
The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) is the apex body for disaster management in India.
Fire accidents are a significant cause of casualties in urban areas due to overcrowding and lack of adherence to safety norms.
The incident highlights the importance of public awareness and regular safety drills.
mainsPoints: Analyze the systemic failures in urban planning and regulatory enforcement that contribute to such fire tragedies in Indian cities.
Discuss the role of government agencies, local bodies, and commercial establishments in ensuring public safety and preventing disasters.
Suggest comprehensive measures, including technological interventions and community participation, to improve fire safety infrastructure and response mechanisms in urban India.
Examine the socio-economic impact of such disasters on affected families and local communities.
articles.imageDescription: Firefighters battling a blaze at a multi-storey building in a crowded urban street.
articles.trivia: Kolkata was the first city in India to have a modern fire brigade, established in 1822.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Deepens Cooperation on Indo-Pacific Security and Green Technologies
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• Both nations pledged to enhance cooperation in maritime security, defense exercises, and intelligence sharing to counter regional challenges.
• A new 'Green Technology Partnership' initiative was launched, focusing on collaboration in renewable energy, hydrogen production, and sustainable urban development.
• Discussions also covered strengthening supply chain resilience and expanding trade and investment ties.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of interests between India and Japan in regional stability and economic growth.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are members of the QUAD grouping, alongside the US and Australia.
Japan is a significant investor in India's infrastructure projects, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.
The 'Free and Open Indo-Pacific' concept is a cornerstone of Japan's foreign policy.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy, making it a key component of green transition.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the India-Japan partnership in shaping the geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss how enhanced cooperation in green technologies can contribute to both nations' climate goals and economic development.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in deepening defense and security cooperation between India and Japan, considering regional dynamics.
Examine the role of bilateral dialogues in fostering trust and mutual understanding amidst complex international relations.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and Japanese flags displayed during a bilateral meeting.
articles.trivia: India and Japan share a long history of cultural exchange, with Buddhism playing a significant role.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-07
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched its latest Earth Observation Satellite, EOS-07, from Sriharikota.
• EOS-07 is equipped with high-resolution optical and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) payloads, designed for advanced imaging and data collection.
• The satellite will provide critical data for various applications, including agriculture, forestry, disaster management, and urban planning.
• This mission further strengthens India's capabilities in remote sensing and enhances its self-reliance in space technology.
• The launch vehicle used was the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV), continuing its reliable track record.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: EOS series satellites are designed for various earth observation applications.
PSLV is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle, primarily used for launching Earth Observation and remote sensing satellites.
SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) can capture images through clouds and at night.
Sriharikota in Andhra Pradesh is India's primary spaceport.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Earth Observation Satellites like EOS-07 for India's economic development, disaster management, and national security.
Analyze how ISRO's advancements in indigenous space technology contribute to India's self-reliance and global standing in the space sector.
Evaluate the potential applications of high-resolution remote sensing data in addressing contemporary challenges such as climate change, urban sprawl, and agricultural productivity.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships in further accelerating India's space program.
articles.imageDescription: A PSLV rocket lifting off from the launch pad against a clear sky.
articles.trivia: ISRO operates one of the largest constellations of remote sensing satellites in the world.

articles.headline: New National Policy on E-Waste Management Aims for Circular Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change unveiled a comprehensive National Policy on E-Waste Management 2026.
• The policy aims to promote a circular economy model for electronic waste, focusing on extended producer responsibility (EPR), recycling, and resource recovery.
• It introduces stricter targets for collection and processing of e-waste, along with incentives for formal sector recyclers.
• The policy also emphasizes public awareness campaigns and skill development for safe e-waste handling.
• This move is critical to address the growing volume of e-waste and its environmental hazards.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: E-waste Rules in India were first notified in 2011, revised in 2016 and 2022.
A circular economy aims to reduce waste and pollution, keep products and materials in use, and regenerate natural systems.
EPR makes producers responsible for the environmental impacts of their products throughout their lifecycle.
E-waste contains toxic substances like lead, mercury, and cadmium.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the features and potential impact of the new National Policy on E-Waste Management in promoting a circular economy in India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing effective e-waste management strategies, particularly concerning the informal sector and public participation.
Suggest innovative solutions and best practices from around the world that India can adopt to improve its e-waste recycling infrastructure.
Examine the environmental and health hazards associated with improper e-waste disposal and the necessity of robust policy frameworks.
articles.imageDescription: A pile of discarded electronic gadgets, symbolizing e-waste, with a green recycling symbol overlay.
articles.trivia: India is one of the top five e-waste generating countries globally.

articles.headline: Government Unveils 'SevaSetu' Portal for Streamlined Public Grievance Redressal
articles.explainer:
• The Central Government launched 'SevaSetu', a unified national portal designed to enhance public grievance redressal and service delivery.
• The portal integrates various state and central government services, offering a single window for citizens to lodge complaints, track status, and access information.
• It leverages AI-based mechanisms for faster processing and routing of grievances to relevant departments.
• 'SevaSetu' aims to improve transparency, accountability, and efficiency in public administration.
• The initiative is part of the government's broader 'Digital India' vision for citizen-centric governance.
gsSubTopics: Governance, Transparency and Accountability and institutional and other measures
e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
prelimsPoints: The Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG) is nodal for public grievances.
Previous initiatives include CPGRAMS (Centralized Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System).
AI integration aims to reduce human intervention and improve efficiency.
Citizen-centric governance focuses on making government services more accessible and responsive to citizens.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the potential of 'SevaSetu' to transform public service delivery and grievance redressal in India, considering previous e-governance initiatives.
Discuss the challenges associated with integrating multiple government services onto a single platform and ensuring accessibility for all citizens.
Analyze how technology, particularly AI, can enhance transparency and accountability in public administration, while also addressing concerns of data privacy and digital divide.
Examine the role of such portals in fostering trust between citizens and the government and improving ease of living.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized graphic of interconnected digital platforms with a central 'SevaSetu' logo.
articles.trivia: India's first e-governance initiatives began in the 1970s with computerization of government departments.

articles.headline: New 'Kisan Samriddhi Yojana' Launched to Boost Farmer Incomes and Promote Sustainable Farming
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare launched the 'Kisan Samriddhi Yojana', a comprehensive scheme aimed at enhancing farmer incomes.
• The scheme provides financial assistance for adopting sustainable agricultural practices, including organic farming, precision agriculture, and climate-resilient crops.
• It also includes provisions for improved market linkages, post-harvest infrastructure, and access to agricultural credit at lower interest rates.
• Skill development programs for farmers on modern farming techniques and value addition are a key component.
• The Yojana seeks to address agrarian distress and promote long-term food security.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management
Land reforms in India.
prelimsPoints: Many government schemes like PM-KISAN, PM Fasal Bima Yojana, and Soil Health Card Scheme aim to support farmers.
Sustainable agriculture focuses on long-term productivity without harming the environment.
Organic farming avoids synthetic fertilizers and pesticides.
Market linkages connect farmers directly to consumers or processors, reducing intermediaries.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the potential of 'Kisan Samriddhi Yojana' to address the multi-faceted challenges faced by Indian farmers and achieve the goal of doubling farmer incomes.
Discuss the importance of integrating sustainable farming practices with market-oriented strategies for long-term agricultural prosperity.
Analyze the role of government interventions in providing financial support, infrastructure, and skill development to empower farmers.
Examine how such schemes contribute to national food security and rural development.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer smiling in a green field, symbolizing prosperity and sustainable farming.
articles.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of agricultural products.

articles.headline: India Joins Multilateral Maritime Exercise 'Varuna Shakti' in Indian Ocean Region
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Navy is participating in 'Varuna Shakti', a major multilateral maritime security exercise in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
• The exercise involves naval forces from several friendly countries, focusing on interoperability, anti-submarine warfare (ASW), and maritime domain awareness.
• 'Varuna Shakti' aims to enhance collective security capabilities and promote regional stability amidst evolving geopolitical scenarios.
• India's participation underscores its commitment to maritime security and its role as a net security provider in the IOR.
• The exercise also includes joint drills for humanitarian assistance and disaster relief (HADR) operations.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: India conducts various bilateral and multilateral naval exercises (e.g., Malabar, Milan, SLINEX).
The Indian Ocean is crucial for global trade and energy security.
HADR operations are a key aspect of naval diplomacy.
Interoperability refers to the ability of different forces to operate together effectively.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of India's participation in multilateral maritime exercises like 'Varuna Shakti' for its security interests and role in the Indian Ocean Region.
Discuss how such exercises contribute to enhancing regional stability, interoperability among naval forces, and collective response capabilities to maritime challenges.
Evaluate India's 'Net Security Provider' role in the IOR and the various initiatives it undertakes to fulfill this role.
Examine the emerging security challenges in the Indian Ocean and how multilateral cooperation can address them effectively.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships from different countries sailing in formation during a maritime exercise.
articles.trivia: The Indian Ocean is the third-largest ocean and covers about 20% of the water on Earth's surface.

articles.headline: Project Tiger Review Shows Mixed Results: Population Stable, Habitat Fragmentation a Growing Concern
articles.explainer:
• A biennial review of Project Tiger revealed a stable tiger population across India, a testament to conservation efforts.
• However, the report highlighted increasing habitat fragmentation and human-wildlife conflict as major threats to long-term tiger survival.
• It called for enhanced corridor connectivity between tiger reserves and community participation in conservation.
• The review emphasized the need for a holistic approach, integrating ecological and socio-economic factors.
• India currently hosts over 70% of the world's wild tiger population across its numerous reserves.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to protect tigers in India.
NTCA is a statutory body under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
Tiger census in India is conducted every four years using the M-STrIPES (Monitoring System for Tigers – Intensive Protection and Ecological Status) methodology.
Habitat fragmentation refers to the emergence of discontinuities in an organism's preferred habitat.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the successes and ongoing challenges of Project Tiger in India, particularly concerning habitat fragmentation and human-wildlife conflict.
Discuss the importance of wildlife corridors and community involvement in ensuring the long-term viability of tiger populations.
Analyze the ecological and economic benefits of tiger conservation and how it contributes to broader biodiversity protection efforts.
Suggest policy measures and technological interventions to mitigate human-wildlife conflict and enhance the effectiveness of conservation strategies.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through a dense forest, symbolizing wildlife conservation.
articles.trivia: The Bengal Tiger (Panthera tigris tigris) is India's national animal.

articles.headline: Indigenous Vaccine for Novel Respiratory Virus Shows Promising Results in Phase 3 Trials
articles.explainer:
• An indigenously developed vaccine for a recently identified novel respiratory virus has demonstrated high efficacy in its Phase 3 clinical trials.
• The vaccine, developed by a collaboration of ICMR and a leading Indian pharmaceutical company, showed strong immune response and safety profile.
• This breakthrough is crucial for enhancing India's preparedness against emerging infectious diseases and reducing dependence on foreign imports.
• The government plans for rapid emergency use authorization and mass production, aiming for widespread availability.
• The success highlights India's growing capabilities in biotechnology and vaccine development.
gsSubTopics: Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Biotechnology
prelimsPoints: ICMR is the apex body in India for the formulation, coordination and promotion of biomedical research.
Phase 3 trials involve thousands of participants to confirm efficacy and monitor adverse reactions.
Emergency Use Authorization allows for the use of unapproved medical products during public health emergencies.
India is often referred to as the 'pharmacy of the world' due to its large-scale vaccine and generic drug production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous vaccine development for India's public health security and its role in global health.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in accelerating vaccine research, development, and manufacturing in India.
Evaluate the ethical considerations and regulatory frameworks necessary for the rapid deployment of new vaccines during health crises.
Examine how advancements in biotechnology can strengthen India's response to future pandemics and emerging infectious diseases.
articles.imageDescription: A scientist in a lab coat holding a vaccine vial, symbolizing medical research and development.
articles.trivia: India's Universal Immunization Programme (UIP) is one of the largest public health programs globally.

articles.headline: ASI Unearths Significant Jain Relics at Ancient Site in Karnataka, Shedding Light on Early Jainism
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of significant Jain relics, including sculptures and inscriptions, at an ancient site near Badami in Karnataka.
• The findings date back to the early medieval period, providing crucial insights into the spread and evolution of Jainism in South India.
• Among the discoveries are rare rock-cut caves and idols of Jain Tirthankaras, indicating a thriving Jain monastic tradition.
• This excavation is expected to contribute immensely to the understanding of India's religious history and architectural heritage.
• The site will be further explored and protected as a national heritage monument.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
The salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
prelimsPoints: Badami was the capital of the early Chalukyas, known for its rock-cut temples.
Jainism is one of India's oldest religions, founded by Rishabhanatha (the first Tirthankara).
ASI is responsible for archaeological research and the protection of cultural heritage.
Karnataka has a rich history of Jainism, with prominent centers like Shravanabelagola.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical significance of Jainism's presence in South India, drawing insights from the recent archaeological discoveries in Karnataka.
Analyze the role of archaeological excavations by ASI in reconstructing India's ancient past and understanding its diverse religious and cultural landscape.
Evaluate the challenges and strategies involved in preserving and promoting such ancient sites for future generations.
Examine the architectural and sculptural styles prevalent in early Jain rock-cut caves and their contribution to Indian art history.
articles.imageDescription: Ancient rock-cut Jain sculptures discovered at an archaeological site.
articles.trivia: The Gomateshwara statue at Shravanabelagola, Karnataka, is one of the largest monolithic statues in the world, dedicated to Bahubali, a revered figure in Jainism.

articles.headline: Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor (EDFC) Phase II Commissioned, Boosting Logistics Efficiency
articles.explainer:
• The second phase of the Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor (EDFC), stretching from Sonnagar (Bihar) to Dankuni (West Bengal), has been successfully commissioned.
• This completion marks a significant milestone in India's efforts to modernize its freight transportation network.
• The EDFC aims to decongest existing railway lines, reduce transit times for goods, and lower logistics costs for industries.
• It is expected to boost economic activity in the eastern states and enhance India's competitiveness in manufacturing and trade.
• The project utilizes advanced signaling and electrification systems for efficient operations.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: India has two major DFCs: Eastern DFC (EDFC) and Western DFC (WDFC).
DFCs are dedicated railway tracks for freight trains, separating them from passenger traffic.
The project is implemented by Dedicated Freight Corridor Corporation of India Limited (DFCCIL).
Reduced logistics costs are crucial for 'Make in India' initiative.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative impact of the Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor on India's logistics sector, industrial growth, and regional development.
Discuss the challenges faced in the implementation of such mega infrastructure projects and how they were overcome.
Evaluate the role of modern railway infrastructure in improving India's global competitiveness and achieving its economic aspirations.
Examine the broader implications of DFCs for environmental sustainability and decongestion of existing railway networks.
articles.imageDescription: A long freight train moving rapidly on a dedicated railway track, symbolizing efficient logistics.
articles.trivia: The total length of the EDFC is approximately 1,856 km, connecting Ludhiana to Dankuni.

articles.headline: Inter-State Council Convenes to Address Fiscal Devolution and Cooperative Federalism Issues
articles.explainer:
• The Inter-State Council convened its latest meeting to deliberate on critical issues pertaining to fiscal devolution and strengthening cooperative federalism in India.
• Chief Ministers and Union Ministers discussed the recommendations of the latest Finance Commission regarding revenue sharing and grants to states.
• Key agenda items included harmonizing state and central policies, dispute resolution mechanisms, and improving inter-state coordination on development projects.
• The meeting underscored the importance of dialogue and consensus-building for a robust federal structure.
• Various states presented their concerns regarding the financial autonomy and implementation of central schemes.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions
Comparison of the Indian constitutional scheme with that of other countries.
prelimsPoints: The Inter-State Council is a non-permanent constitutional body established under Article 263 of the Indian Constitution.
It is chaired by the Prime Minister and includes Chief Ministers of all states and Union Ministers.
Its mandate is to investigate and discuss subjects of common interest between the Union and the states.
The Sarkaria Commission recommended the establishment of a permanent Inter-State Council.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional mandate and significance of the Inter-State Council in fostering cooperative federalism and resolving Centre-State disputes in India.
Analyze the contemporary challenges in fiscal devolution and Centre-State financial relations, as deliberated in the recent meeting.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Inter-State Council as a mechanism for policy coordination and consensus-building among diverse political entities.
Suggest reforms to strengthen the functioning of the Inter-State Council to make it a more dynamic forum for federal dialogue.
articles.imageDescription: A round table conference with various state and union representatives, symbolizing inter-state dialogue.
articles.trivia: The Inter-State Council was formally constituted in 1990 following the recommendations of the Sarkaria Commission.

articles.headline: Government Reviews Progress on Left-Wing Extremism Strategy, Emphasizes Development and Security Synergy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs conducted a high-level review of the government's strategy to counter Left-Wing Extremism (LWE) in affected regions.
• The review highlighted significant progress in reducing the geographical spread and intensity of LWE violence, attributing success to a multi-pronged approach.
• Emphasis was placed on further strengthening the synergy between security operations and developmental initiatives, particularly in providing infrastructure and basic services.
• The government reiterated its 'zero-tolerance' policy towards extremism while ensuring human rights protection.
• Special focus areas include capacity building of local police forces and engaging with tribal communities.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism; Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: LWE is primarily concentrated in the 'Red Corridor' regions of central and eastern India.
The government's strategy includes both security-centric measures and socio-economic development.
Developmental initiatives aim to address the root causes of extremism, such as poverty and lack of opportunities.
Special forces like COBRA (Commando Battalion for Resolute Action) are deployed in LWE-affected areas.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the 'development and security synergy' approach adopted by the government to combat Left-Wing Extremism in India.
Discuss the socio-economic factors that contribute to the spread of LWE and how developmental interventions can effectively address them.
Analyze the role of security forces in maintaining law and order in LWE-affected regions while ensuring the protection of human rights.
Examine the challenges in integrating tribal communities into mainstream development and preventing their exploitation by extremist groups.
articles.imageDescription: A map of India highlighting areas affected by Left-Wing Extremism, with symbols of development and security.
articles.trivia: The term 'Naxalite' originated from the Naxalbari village in West Bengal, where a peasant uprising took place in 1967.

articles.headline: National Quantum Mission Reports Progress in Quantum Computing and Communication
articles.explainer:
• The National Quantum Mission (NQM) released its first progress report, highlighting advancements in quantum computing, communication, and sensing technologies.
• Indian researchers have made strides in developing quantum algorithms and building prototypes of quantum processors.
• Significant progress was also reported in quantum key distribution (QKD) for secure communication and developing atomic clocks for precise timing.
• The mission aims to make India a leading nation in quantum technology development by 2030.
• Investment in talent development and international collaborations remain key priorities.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: The NQM was approved in 2023 with a total outlay of Rs 6,003.65 crore.
Quantum computing uses quantum-mechanical phenomena like superposition and entanglement.
QKD provides ultra-secure communication by using quantum mechanics to detect eavesdropping.
Quantum technologies have applications in healthcare, finance, defense, and space.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Quantum Mission for India's technological sovereignty and global competitiveness.
Analyze the potential applications of quantum computing, communication, and sensing across various sectors, including defense and finance.
Evaluate the challenges in developing a robust quantum ecosystem in India, encompassing research, infrastructure, and skilled workforce.
Examine the ethical and security implications of quantum technologies and the need for appropriate policy frameworks.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic graphic representing quantum entanglement and interconnected qubits.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'quantum' refers to the smallest possible discrete unit of any physical property.

articles.headline: ISRO's GSAT-12R Successfully Completes In-Orbit Operations, Enhancing Telecommunication Services
articles.explainer:
• ISRO announced that its communication satellite GSAT-12R has successfully completed all in-orbit operations and is fully operational.
• The satellite, launched earlier, is now providing enhanced telecommunication services, including DTH television, VSAT services, and digital satellite news gathering.
• GSAT-12R is a replacement for the INSAT-2E/GSAT-12 satellite, ensuring continuity and improvement of services.
• This successful deployment further augments India's satellite communication infrastructure and digital connectivity.
• It will play a crucial role in bridging the digital divide, especially in remote and rural areas.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: GSAT series satellites are India's indigenous communication satellites.
Geosynchronous satellites orbit at the same rate as the Earth's rotation, appearing stationary from the ground.
DTH services provide television directly to subscribers via satellite.
VSATs are small earth stations that can send and receive data via satellite.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of communication satellites like GSAT-12R in strengthening India's digital infrastructure and promoting socio-economic development.
Analyze how enhanced telecommunication services can help bridge the digital divide and improve connectivity in remote areas.
Evaluate the strategic importance of indigenous satellite development for India's self-reliance in space technology and national security.
Examine the various applications of communication satellites in modern society, beyond just television and internet services.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of a communication satellite orbiting Earth.
articles.trivia: India's first experimental communication satellite was APPLE (Ariane Passenger Payload Experiment) launched in 1981.

highlights: Supreme Court clarifies legislative immunity for MPs/MLAs, emphasizing accountability in corruption cases.
Economic Survey 2025-26 projects robust GDP growth for India, highlighting green transition priorities.
Tragic fire at a Kolkata restaurant claims 25 lives, sparking concerns over urban fire safety norms.
India and Japan deepen strategic cooperation on Indo-Pacific security and green technologies.
ISRO successfully launches advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-07 for diverse applications.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to legislative immunity in India, consider the following statements:
1. Article 105 of the Indian Constitution grants immunity to Members of Parliament from proceedings in any court in respect of anything said or any vote given by him in Parliament.
2. The recent Supreme Court ruling has held that this immunity extends to acts of bribery or corruption related to legislative actions.
3. Article 194 provides similar privileges to members of State Legislatures.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. Article 105(2) grants immunity to MPs for anything said or any vote given in Parliament. Statement 2 is incorrect. The recent Supreme Court ruling (as per the ePaper) explicitly clarified that this immunity *does not* extend to acts of bribery or corruption related to such actions. Statement 3 is correct. Article 194 provides similar powers, privileges, and immunities to members of State Legislatures.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for preparing and presenting the Economic Survey in India?
options: (c) Department of Economic Affairs, Ministry of Finance
(d) Chief Economic Adviser (CEA), Ministry of Finance
prelimsMocks.answer: (d) Chief Economic Adviser (CEA), Ministry of Finance
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Economic Survey is prepared by the Chief Economic Adviser (CEA) in the Ministry of Finance and is presented by the Finance Minister in Parliament, typically a day before the Union Budget. While the Department of Economic Affairs is part of the Ministry of Finance, the specific responsibility for drafting the Economic Survey rests with the CEA.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the National Policy on E-Waste Management 2026:
1. It primarily aims to promote a linear economy model for electronic waste.
2. It introduces Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) as a key mechanism.
3. It focuses on stricter targets for collection and processing of e-waste.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The policy aims to promote a *circular economy* model, not a linear one. Statement 2 is correct, as EPR is a central pillar of e-waste management policies. Statement 3 is also correct, as the policy introduces stricter targets for collection and processing.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is the primary launch vehicle used by ISRO for launching Earth Observation and remote sensing satellites?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV) is ISRO's workhorse for launching Earth Observation and remote sensing satellites into polar sun-synchronous orbits. GSLV is primarily used for heavier communication satellites into geosynchronous transfer orbit. SSLV is for small satellites, and RLV is a technology demonstrator.

prelimsMocks.question: The Inter-State Council in India is a constitutional body established under which Article of the Constitution?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Inter-State Council is established under Article 263 of the Indian Constitution to inquire into, and advise upon, disputes which may have arisen between States; to investigate and discuss subjects in which some or all of the States, or the Union and one or more of the States, have a common interest; and to make recommendations upon any such subject and for the better co-ordination of policy and action with respect to that subject.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on legislative immunity for parliamentary accountability and anti-corruption efforts in India. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these; Governance, Transparency and Accountability.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the SC ruling and its context (Articles 105/194). Explain how it redefines the boundary of immunity, not protecting corruption. Discuss its positive implications for accountability, electoral ethics, and anti-corruption agencies. Briefly mention potential challenges or debates on legislative independence.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the challenges and opportunities for India-Japan cooperation in ensuring Indo-Pacific security and fostering green technological advancements. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the strategic convergence between India and Japan in the Indo-Pacific. For security, discuss shared concerns (e.g., China's assertiveness), joint exercises, and QUAD. For green tech, highlight areas like renewable energy, hydrogen, and climate resilience. Address challenges such as differing strategic priorities (e.g., QUAD vs. bilateral), funding for green tech, and geopolitical complexities. Conclude with the immense potential of this partnership.

mainsMocks.question: Critically evaluate India's strategies for managing Left-Wing Extremism, considering the synergy between development and security. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Linkages between development and spread of extremism; Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism; Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by defining LWE and its root causes (socio-economic deprivation). Explain the government's dual strategy of 'security and development'. Discuss the successes of security operations (reduced violence, geographical spread) and developmental initiatives (infrastructure, schemes). Critically evaluate challenges such as implementation gaps, human rights concerns, continued recruitment, and the need for deeper engagement with local communities. Conclude with recommendations for a more holistic and sustainable approach.

quickBytes.text: January 30 is observed as Martyr's Day in India, commemorating Mahatma Gandhi's assassination.
The Economic Survey is presented annually in Parliament, a day before the Union Budget, providing an economic review.
Article 105 of the Indian Constitution deals with the powers, privileges, etc., of Members of Parliament.
A circular economy aims to reduce waste and pollution, keep products in use, and regenerate natural systems.
ISRO's PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is known as its 'workhorse' for launching Earth Observation Satellites.
Project Tiger, launched in 1973, is a major wildlife conservation initiative in India.
The Inter-State Council is a constitutional body established under Article 263 to foster Centre-State cooperation.
Dedicated Freight Corridors (DFCs) are specialized railway lines for freight, aiming to reduce logistics costs.
Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) utilizes quantum mechanics to ensure highly secure communication channels.
The term 'Naxalite' originated from the Naxalbari village in West Bengal, associated with Left-Wing Extremism.

GS1.headline: ASI Unearths Significant Jain Relics at Ancient Site in Karnataka, Shedding Light on Early Jainism
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of significant Jain relics, including sculptures and inscriptions, at an ancient site near Badami in Karnataka.
• The findings date back to the early medieval period, providing crucial insights into the spread and evolution of Jainism in South India.
• Among the discoveries are rare rock-cut caves and idols of Jain Tirthankaras, indicating a thriving Jain monastic tradition.
• This excavation is expected to contribute immensely to the understanding of India's religious history and architectural heritage.
• The site will be further explored and protected as a national heritage monument.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
The salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
prelimsPoints: Badami was the capital of the early Chalukyas, known for its rock-cut temples.
Jainism is one of India's oldest religions, founded by Rishabhanatha (the first Tirthankara).
ASI is responsible for archaeological research and the protection of cultural heritage.
Karnataka has a rich history of Jainism, with prominent centers like Shravanabelagola.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical significance of Jainism's presence in South India, drawing insights from the recent archaeological discoveries in Karnataka.
Analyze the role of archaeological excavations by ASI in reconstructing India's ancient past and understanding its diverse religious and cultural landscape.
Evaluate the challenges and strategies involved in preserving and promoting such ancient sites for future generations.
Examine the architectural and sculptural styles prevalent in early Jain rock-cut caves and their contribution to Indian art history.
GS1.imageDescription: Ancient rock-cut Jain sculptures discovered at an archaeological site.
GS1.trivia: The Gomateshwara statue at Shravanabelagola, Karnataka, is one of the largest monolithic statues in the world, dedicated to Bahubali, a revered figure in Jainism.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Clarifies Scope of Legislative Immunity for MPs/MLAs in Corruption Cases
GS2.explainer:
• A landmark Supreme Court ruling today delimited the scope of legislative immunity provided under Articles 105(2) and 194(2) of the Constitution.
• The Court held that immunity against prosecution for speeches and votes within the legislature does not extend to acts of bribery or corruption related to such actions.
• The judgment emphasizes the need for accountability and transparency among elected representatives.
• This decision is expected to have significant implications for anti-corruption efforts and the conduct of legislative business.
• It follows a series of petitions challenging the interpretation of parliamentary privileges in cases involving alleged 'cash-for-vote' or 'cash-for-query' scams.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Governance, Transparency and Accountability
prelimsPoints: Articles 105 and 194 of the Indian Constitution deal with powers, privileges, etc., of Members of Parliament and State Legislatures, respectively.
The current ruling distinguishes between legitimate legislative acts and acts of corruption.
The concept of 'privilege motion' and 'breach of privilege' are related to legislative immunity.
The judgement re-affirms the principle of accountability for public servants.
mainsPoints: Discuss the balance between ensuring legislative freedom and holding elected representatives accountable for corrupt practices.
Analyze the implications of this judgment on parliamentary conduct, electoral ethics, and the fight against corruption in India.
Evaluate how this ruling strengthens democratic principles by promoting transparency and integrity in legislative bodies.
Examine the historical context of parliamentary privileges and how judicial interpretations have shaped their application over time.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel striking a sound block in front of the Supreme Court of India building.
GS2.trivia: The first 'cash-for-vote' scandal in India dates back to 1993, involving the Narasimha Rao government.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Powers, Privileges and Immunities of MPs/MLAs

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Deepens Cooperation on Indo-Pacific Security and Green Technologies
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• Both nations pledged to enhance cooperation in maritime security, defense exercises, and intelligence sharing to counter regional challenges.
• A new 'Green Technology Partnership' initiative was launched, focusing on collaboration in renewable energy, hydrogen production, and sustainable urban development.
• Discussions also covered strengthening supply chain resilience and expanding trade and investment ties.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of interests between India and Japan in regional stability and economic growth.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are members of the QUAD grouping, alongside the US and Australia.
Japan is a significant investor in India's infrastructure projects, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.
The 'Free and Open Indo-Pacific' concept is a cornerstone of Japan's foreign policy.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy, making it a key component of green transition.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the India-Japan partnership in shaping the geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss how enhanced cooperation in green technologies can contribute to both nations' climate goals and economic development.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in deepening defense and security cooperation between India and Japan, considering regional dynamics.
Examine the role of bilateral dialogues in fostering trust and mutual understanding amidst complex international relations.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and Japanese flags displayed during a bilateral meeting.
GS2.trivia: India and Japan share a long history of cultural exchange, with Buddhism playing a significant role.

GS2.headline: Government Unveils 'SevaSetu' Portal for Streamlined Public Grievance Redressal
GS2.explainer:
• The Central Government launched 'SevaSetu', a unified national portal designed to enhance public grievance redressal and service delivery.
• The portal integrates various state and central government services, offering a single window for citizens to lodge complaints, track status, and access information.
• It leverages AI-based mechanisms for faster processing and routing of grievances to relevant departments.
• 'SevaSetu' aims to improve transparency, accountability, and efficiency in public administration.
• The initiative is part of the government's broader 'Digital India' vision for citizen-centric governance.
gsSubTopics: Governance, Transparency and Accountability and institutional and other measures
e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
prelimsPoints: The Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG) is nodal for public grievances.
Previous initiatives include CPGRAMS (Centralized Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System).
AI integration aims to reduce human intervention and improve efficiency.
Citizen-centric governance focuses on making government services more accessible and responsive to citizens.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the potential of 'SevaSetu' to transform public service delivery and grievance redressal in India, considering previous e-governance initiatives.
Discuss the challenges associated with integrating multiple government services onto a single platform and ensuring accessibility for all citizens.
Analyze how technology, particularly AI, can enhance transparency and accountability in public administration, while also addressing concerns of data privacy and digital divide.
Examine the role of such portals in fostering trust between citizens and the government and improving ease of living.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized graphic of interconnected digital platforms with a central 'SevaSetu' logo.
GS2.trivia: India's first e-governance initiatives began in the 1970s with computerization of government departments.

GS2.headline: Inter-State Council Convenes to Address Fiscal Devolution and Cooperative Federalism Issues
GS2.explainer:
• The Inter-State Council convened its latest meeting to deliberate on critical issues pertaining to fiscal devolution and strengthening cooperative federalism in India.
• Chief Ministers and Union Ministers discussed the recommendations of the latest Finance Commission regarding revenue sharing and grants to states.
• Key agenda items included harmonizing state and central policies, dispute resolution mechanisms, and improving inter-state coordination on development projects.
• The meeting underscored the importance of dialogue and consensus-building for a robust federal structure.
• Various states presented their concerns regarding the financial autonomy and implementation of central schemes.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions
Comparison of the Indian constitutional scheme with that of other countries.
prelimsPoints: The Inter-State Council is a non-permanent constitutional body established under Article 263 of the Indian Constitution.
It is chaired by the Prime Minister and includes Chief Ministers of all states and Union Ministers.
Its mandate is to investigate and discuss subjects of common interest between the Union and the states.
The Sarkaria Commission recommended the establishment of a permanent Inter-State Council.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional mandate and significance of the Inter-State Council in fostering cooperative federalism and resolving Centre-State disputes in India.
Analyze the contemporary challenges in fiscal devolution and Centre-State financial relations, as deliberated in the recent meeting.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Inter-State Council as a mechanism for policy coordination and consensus-building among diverse political entities.
Suggest reforms to strengthen the functioning of the Inter-State Council to make it a more dynamic forum for federal dialogue.
GS2.imageDescription: A round table conference with various state and union representatives, symbolizing inter-state dialogue.
GS2.trivia: The Inter-State Council was formally constituted in 1990 following the recommendations of the Sarkaria Commission.

GS3.headline: Economic Survey 2025-26 Projects Robust Growth Amidst Global Headwinds, Focus on Green Transition
GS3.explainer:
• The annual Economic Survey, presented a day before the Union Budget, projected India's GDP growth at 7.2-7.8% for the upcoming fiscal year.
• It highlighted resilient domestic demand, public capital expenditure, and strong services sector performance as key growth drivers.
• The survey called for increased investment in green technologies and sustainable infrastructure to achieve net-zero targets.
• It also emphasized the need for continued structural reforms in agriculture, labour, and logistics to enhance productivity and competitiveness.
• Inflation management, fiscal consolidation, and job creation were identified as critical policy priorities.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Investment models
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Economic Survey is prepared by the Chief Economic Adviser (CEA) and presented by the Finance Minister.
It provides a detailed report on the state of the Indian economy in the financial year gone by.
The survey often outlines policy recommendations and future outlook.
Green transition involves shifting to sustainable and low-carbon economic activities.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key recommendations of the Economic Survey 2025-26 regarding India's path to a sustainable and high-growth economy.
Discuss the challenges posed by global headwinds and domestic structural issues in achieving the projected GDP growth targets.
Evaluate the role of public capital expenditure and green investments in driving India's economic recovery and long-term development.
Examine the interlinkages between economic growth, environmental sustainability, and social equity as highlighted in the survey.
GS3.imageDescription: A graph showing India's projected GDP growth curve with green energy infrastructure in the background.
GS3.trivia: The first Economic Survey was presented in 1950-51 and was part of the Union Budget documents until 1964.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-07
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched its latest Earth Observation Satellite, EOS-07, from Sriharikota.
• EOS-07 is equipped with high-resolution optical and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) payloads, designed for advanced imaging and data collection.
• The satellite will provide critical data for various applications, including agriculture, forestry, disaster management, and urban planning.
• This mission further strengthens India's capabilities in remote sensing and enhances its self-reliance in space technology.
• The launch vehicle used was the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV), continuing its reliable track record.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: EOS series satellites are designed for various earth observation applications.
PSLV is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle, primarily used for launching Earth Observation and remote sensing satellites.
SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) can capture images through clouds and at night.
Sriharikota in Andhra Pradesh is India's primary spaceport.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Earth Observation Satellites like EOS-07 for India's economic development, disaster management, and national security.
Analyze how ISRO's advancements in indigenous space technology contribute to India's self-reliance and global standing in the space sector.
Evaluate the potential applications of high-resolution remote sensing data in addressing contemporary challenges such as climate change, urban sprawl, and agricultural productivity.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships in further accelerating India's space program.
GS3.imageDescription: A PSLV rocket lifting off from the launch pad against a clear sky.
GS3.trivia: ISRO operates one of the largest constellations of remote sensing satellites in the world.

GS3.headline: New National Policy on E-Waste Management Aims for Circular Economy
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change unveiled a comprehensive National Policy on E-Waste Management 2026.
• The policy aims to promote a circular economy model for electronic waste, focusing on extended producer responsibility (EPR), recycling, and resource recovery.
• It introduces stricter targets for collection and processing of e-waste, along with incentives for formal sector recyclers.
• The policy also emphasizes public awareness campaigns and skill development for safe e-waste handling.
• This move is critical to address the growing volume of e-waste and its environmental hazards.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: E-waste Rules in India were first notified in 2011, revised in 2016 and 2022.
A circular economy aims to reduce waste and pollution, keep products and materials in use, and regenerate natural systems.
EPR makes producers responsible for the environmental impacts of their products throughout their lifecycle.
E-waste contains toxic substances like lead, mercury, and cadmium.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the features and potential impact of the new National Policy on E-Waste Management in promoting a circular economy in India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing effective e-waste management strategies, particularly concerning the informal sector and public participation.
Suggest innovative solutions and best practices from around the world that India can adopt to improve its e-waste recycling infrastructure.
Examine the environmental and health hazards associated with improper e-waste disposal and the necessity of robust policy frameworks.
GS3.imageDescription: A pile of discarded electronic gadgets, symbolizing e-waste, with a green recycling symbol overlay.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the top five e-waste generating countries globally.

GS3.headline: New 'Kisan Samriddhi Yojana' Launched to Boost Farmer Incomes and Promote Sustainable Farming
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare launched the 'Kisan Samriddhi Yojana', a comprehensive scheme aimed at enhancing farmer incomes.
• The scheme provides financial assistance for adopting sustainable agricultural practices, including organic farming, precision agriculture, and climate-resilient crops.
• It also includes provisions for improved market linkages, post-harvest infrastructure, and access to agricultural credit at lower interest rates.
• Skill development programs for farmers on modern farming techniques and value addition are a key component.
• The Yojana seeks to address agrarian distress and promote long-term food security.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management
Land reforms in India.
prelimsPoints: Many government schemes like PM-KISAN, PM Fasal Bima Yojana, and Soil Health Card Scheme aim to support farmers.
Sustainable agriculture focuses on long-term productivity without harming the environment.
Organic farming avoids synthetic fertilizers and pesticides.
Market linkages connect farmers directly to consumers or processors, reducing intermediaries.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the potential of 'Kisan Samriddhi Yojana' to address the multi-faceted challenges faced by Indian farmers and achieve the goal of doubling farmer incomes.
Discuss the importance of integrating sustainable farming practices with market-oriented strategies for long-term agricultural prosperity.
Analyze the role of government interventions in providing financial support, infrastructure, and skill development to empower farmers.
Examine how such schemes contribute to national food security and rural development.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer smiling in a green field, symbolizing prosperity and sustainable farming.
GS3.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of agricultural products.

GS3.headline: Project Tiger Review Shows Mixed Results: Population Stable, Habitat Fragmentation a Growing Concern
GS3.explainer:
• A biennial review of Project Tiger revealed a stable tiger population across India, a testament to conservation efforts.
• However, the report highlighted increasing habitat fragmentation and human-wildlife conflict as major threats to long-term tiger survival.
• It called for enhanced corridor connectivity between tiger reserves and community participation in conservation.
• The review emphasized the need for a holistic approach, integrating ecological and socio-economic factors.
• India currently hosts over 70% of the world's wild tiger population across its numerous reserves.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to protect tigers in India.
NTCA is a statutory body under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
Tiger census in India is conducted every four years using the M-STrIPES (Monitoring System for Tigers – Intensive Protection and Ecological Status) methodology.
Habitat fragmentation refers to the emergence of discontinuities in an organism's preferred habitat.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the successes and ongoing challenges of Project Tiger in India, particularly concerning habitat fragmentation and human-wildlife conflict.
Discuss the importance of wildlife corridors and community involvement in ensuring the long-term viability of tiger populations.
Analyze the ecological and economic benefits of tiger conservation and how it contributes to broader biodiversity protection efforts.
Suggest policy measures and technological interventions to mitigate human-wildlife conflict and enhance the effectiveness of conservation strategies.
GS3.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through a dense forest, symbolizing wildlife conservation.
GS3.trivia: The Bengal Tiger (Panthera tigris tigris) is India's national animal.

GS3.headline: Indigenous Vaccine for Novel Respiratory Virus Shows Promising Results in Phase 3 Trials
GS3.explainer:
• An indigenously developed vaccine for a recently identified novel respiratory virus has demonstrated high efficacy in its Phase 3 clinical trials.
• The vaccine, developed by a collaboration of ICMR and a leading Indian pharmaceutical company, showed strong immune response and safety profile.
• This breakthrough is crucial for enhancing India's preparedness against emerging infectious diseases and reducing dependence on foreign imports.
• The government plans for rapid emergency use authorization and mass production, aiming for widespread availability.
• The success highlights India's growing capabilities in biotechnology and vaccine development.
gsSubTopics: Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Biotechnology
prelimsPoints: ICMR is the apex body in India for the formulation, coordination and promotion of biomedical research.
Phase 3 trials involve thousands of participants to confirm efficacy and monitor adverse reactions.
Emergency Use Authorization allows for the use of unapproved medical products during public health emergencies.
India is often referred to as the 'pharmacy of the world' due to its large-scale vaccine and generic drug production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous vaccine development for India's public health security and its role in global health.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in accelerating vaccine research, development, and manufacturing in India.
Evaluate the ethical considerations and regulatory frameworks necessary for the rapid deployment of new vaccines during health crises.
Examine how advancements in biotechnology can strengthen India's response to future pandemics and emerging infectious diseases.
GS3.imageDescription: A scientist in a lab coat holding a vaccine vial, symbolizing medical research and development.
GS3.trivia: India's Universal Immunization Programme (UIP) is one of the largest public health programs globally.

GS3.headline: Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor (EDFC) Phase II Commissioned, Boosting Logistics Efficiency
GS3.explainer:
• The second phase of the Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor (EDFC), stretching from Sonnagar (Bihar) to Dankuni (West Bengal), has been successfully commissioned.
• This completion marks a significant milestone in India's efforts to modernize its freight transportation network.
• The EDFC aims to decongest existing railway lines, reduce transit times for goods, and lower logistics costs for industries.
• It is expected to boost economic activity in the eastern states and enhance India's competitiveness in manufacturing and trade.
• The project utilizes advanced signaling and electrification systems for efficient operations.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: India has two major DFCs: Eastern DFC (EDFC) and Western DFC (WDFC).
DFCs are dedicated railway tracks for freight trains, separating them from passenger traffic.
The project is implemented by Dedicated Freight Corridor Corporation of India Limited (DFCCIL).
Reduced logistics costs are crucial for 'Make in India' initiative.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative impact of the Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor on India's logistics sector, industrial growth, and regional development.
Discuss the challenges faced in the implementation of such mega infrastructure projects and how they were overcome.
Evaluate the role of modern railway infrastructure in improving India's global competitiveness and achieving its economic aspirations.
Examine the broader implications of DFCs for environmental sustainability and decongestion of existing railway networks.
GS3.imageDescription: A long freight train moving rapidly on a dedicated railway track, symbolizing efficient logistics.
GS3.trivia: The total length of the EDFC is approximately 1,856 km, connecting Ludhiana to Dankuni.

GS3.headline: Government Reviews Progress on Left-Wing Extremism Strategy, Emphasizes Development and Security Synergy
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs conducted a high-level review of the government's strategy to counter Left-Wing Extremism (LWE) in affected regions.
• The review highlighted significant progress in reducing the geographical spread and intensity of LWE violence, attributing success to a multi-pronged approach.
• Emphasis was placed on further strengthening the synergy between security operations and developmental initiatives, particularly in providing infrastructure and basic services.
• The government reiterated its 'zero-tolerance' policy towards extremism while ensuring human rights protection.
• Special focus areas include capacity building of local police forces and engaging with tribal communities.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism; Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: LWE is primarily concentrated in the 'Red Corridor' regions of central and eastern India.
The government's strategy includes both security-centric measures and socio-economic development.
Developmental initiatives aim to address the root causes of extremism, such as poverty and lack of opportunities.
Special forces like COBRA (Commando Battalion for Resolute Action) are deployed in LWE-affected areas.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the 'development and security synergy' approach adopted by the government to combat Left-Wing Extremism in India.
Discuss the socio-economic factors that contribute to the spread of LWE and how developmental interventions can effectively address them.
Analyze the role of security forces in maintaining law and order in LWE-affected regions while ensuring the protection of human rights.
Examine the challenges in integrating tribal communities into mainstream development and preventing their exploitation by extremist groups.
GS3.imageDescription: A map of India highlighting areas affected by Left-Wing Extremism, with symbols of development and security.
GS3.trivia: The term 'Naxalite' originated from the Naxalbari village in West Bengal, where a peasant uprising took place in 1967.

GS3.headline: National Quantum Mission Reports Progress in Quantum Computing and Communication
GS3.explainer:
• The National Quantum Mission (NQM) released its first progress report, highlighting advancements in quantum computing, communication, and sensing technologies.
• Indian researchers have made strides in developing quantum algorithms and building prototypes of quantum processors.
• Significant progress was also reported in quantum key distribution (QKD) for secure communication and developing atomic clocks for precise timing.
• The mission aims to make India a leading nation in quantum technology development by 2030.
• Investment in talent development and international collaborations remain key priorities.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: The NQM was approved in 2023 with a total outlay of Rs 6,003.65 crore.
Quantum computing uses quantum-mechanical phenomena like superposition and entanglement.
QKD provides ultra-secure communication by using quantum mechanics to detect eavesdropping.
Quantum technologies have applications in healthcare, finance, defense, and space.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Quantum Mission for India's technological sovereignty and global competitiveness.
Analyze the potential applications of quantum computing, communication, and sensing across various sectors, including defense and finance.
Evaluate the challenges in developing a robust quantum ecosystem in India, encompassing research, infrastructure, and skilled workforce.
Examine the ethical and security implications of quantum technologies and the need for appropriate policy frameworks.
GS3.imageDescription: A futuristic graphic representing quantum entanglement and interconnected qubits.
GS3.trivia: The concept of 'quantum' refers to the smallest possible discrete unit of any physical property.

GS3.headline: ISRO's GSAT-12R Successfully Completes In-Orbit Operations, Enhancing Telecommunication Services
GS3.explainer:
• ISRO announced that its communication satellite GSAT-12R has successfully completed all in-orbit operations and is fully operational.
• The satellite, launched earlier, is now providing enhanced telecommunication services, including DTH television, VSAT services, and digital satellite news gathering.
• GSAT-12R is a replacement for the INSAT-2E/GSAT-12 satellite, ensuring continuity and improvement of services.
• This successful deployment further augments India's satellite communication infrastructure and digital connectivity.
• It will play a crucial role in bridging the digital divide, especially in remote and rural areas.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: GSAT series satellites are India's indigenous communication satellites.
Geosynchronous satellites orbit at the same rate as the Earth's rotation, appearing stationary from the ground.
DTH services provide television directly to subscribers via satellite.
VSATs are small earth stations that can send and receive data via satellite.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of communication satellites like GSAT-12R in strengthening India's digital infrastructure and promoting socio-economic development.
Analyze how enhanced telecommunication services can help bridge the digital divide and improve connectivity in remote areas.
Evaluate the strategic importance of indigenous satellite development for India's self-reliance in space technology and national security.
Examine the various applications of communication satellites in modern society, beyond just television and internet services.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of a communication satellite orbiting Earth.
GS3.trivia: India's first experimental communication satellite was APPLE (Ariane Passenger Payload Experiment) launched in 1981.

sources: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
